methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $20M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $250M - $2.4B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Liquid AI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series A), post-money $2.35B

private.name: Field AI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series A), post-money $2B

private.name: Twelve Labs
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series A), post-money $250M

data.narrative:
Gladia is a Series A-stage AI & Software company that has raised $20M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of three same-sector, same-stage comparables (Liquid AI at $2.4B, Field AI at $2.0B, and Twelve Labs at $250M), supplemented by a broader universe of 9 private and 1 public AI infrastructure and software peers.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $20M total raised as a proxy for current valuation and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement, yielding $40M. Peer-median analysis across the three direct comparables produces a median valuation of $2.0B, with a range spanning $250M to $2.4B. This wide dispersion reflects heterogeneity in business model maturity and market positioning within the Series A AI software category.

The two methods yield a triangulated range of $34M to $2.3B, with a median of $1.0B. The stage-progression result sits at the low end, reflecting conservative assumptions anchored to deployed capital. The peer-median approach dominates the valuation distribution. Key uncertainties include revenue traction and product differentiation relative to Liquid AI and Field AI, as well as relative positioning against the low-valuation benchmark set by Twelve Labs. This wide range reflects material uncertainty in early-stage AI software valuations.
